title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
summary: Matt Pocock outlines a skill checklist framework for building high-quality AI agent skills, addressing "skill hell" through structured guidance on triggers (user vs model-invoked), structure (steps and references), steering (guiding agent behavior with leading words), and pruning (removing irrelevant material). The framework aims to help developers create maintainable and effective skills by reducing context load and unpredictability.
writeup: Matt Pocock presents a skill checklist framework for building AI agent skills, aimed at avoiding "skill hell." The framework covers four areas: triggers (distinguishing user-invoked from model-invoked skills), structure (organizing steps and references), steering (guiding agent behavior with leading words), and pruning (cutting irrelevant material). The goal is more maintainable and effective skills that reduce context load and unpredictability.
